title: Neomirandea standleyi
intro: Neomirandea standleyi is a plant species within the Lamiaceae family, commonly known as the mint family. The plant has been traditionally used for its potential health benefits, including its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues and wounds. The bioactive constituents of Neomirandea standleyi include diterpenoids, sesquiterpenoids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Neomirandea standleyi is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are employed to address various health concerns.
taxonomy: Neomirandea standleyi is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is native to Central America, specifically in Costa Rica. The classification of Neomirandea standleyi is as follows: it belongs to the kingdom Plantae, phylum Magnoliophyta, class Magnoliopsida, order Lamiales, family Rubiaceae, genus Neomirandea, and species N. standleyi. The genus Neomirandea is part of the tribe Psychotrieae within the Rubiaceae family. Further research may be needed to confirm the exact classification of Neomirandea standleyi.
